items.text:
STOP PHOTOCOPYING YOUR GHANA CARD — IT'S NOW A CRIMINAL OFFENCE. 🪪

The NIA has gazetted new rules: biometric verification is now the only lawful way to verify a Ghana Card for any transaction.

That photocopy the bank agent, the office, or the SIM seller asks for? Now against the law — to demand it and to make it. Even eyeballing the card for verification is out.

And the fines are real: GH¢6,000 to GH¢24,000 for institutions, GH¢600 to GH¢6,000 for individuals.

Why? The NIA says photocopied cards are feeding identity theft.

(Source: Graphic Online | Pulse | Adomonline | Rainbow Radio)
#GreaterNews #Ghana #GhanaCard #NIA

Has anyone asked YOU for a Ghana Card photocopy this week? 👇

Photocopying a Ghana Card for transactions is now a criminal offence — biometric verification is the only lawful check under newly gazetted NIA rules. Fines up to GH¢24,000 for institutions. (Source: Graphic/Pulse) #GreaterNews #Ghana #GhanaCard

PLOT TWIST: QUEIROZ ISN'T GOING ANYWHERE. ⚽🇬🇭

After the emotional goodbye message, after a week of coach-hunt drama — Carlos Queiroz is now set to sign a new two-year deal as Black Stars coach.

The contract, backed by the GFA and the Ministry of Sports, would keep him through the 2027 AFCON. Queiroz has been clear he never resigned — "I'm still the coach," he said.

Sports Minister Kofi Adams says he's behind the two-year deal. The mission if it's signed: end Ghana's long wait for a continental title.

The ink isn't dry yet — signing is expected in the coming days.

(Source: NewsGhana | 3News | Graphic Online | GBC)
#GreaterNews #Ghana #BlackStars #Queiroz

Right call or wrong call? Be honest. 👇

U-turn: Carlos Queiroz is set to sign a NEW two-year Black Stars deal through AFCON 2027 — backed by the GFA and Sports Ministry, per multiple reports. Not signed yet. (Source: NewsGhana/3News/Graphic) #GreaterNews #BlackStars

US AND IRAN TRADE STRIKES OVER THE WORLD'S BUSIEST OIL ROUTE. 🌍

The July 8 truce between the US and Iran has broken down. The US says it carried out a second wave of strikes on Wednesday, aimed at stopping Iran from attacking ships in the Strait of Hormuz — the channel that carries a huge share of the world's oil.

Iran's Revolutionary Guard is threatening to shut the strait entirely. Oil prices jumped, then steadied.

Why it matters here: a prolonged crisis on that route can push global crude — and eventually fuel prices — up. We're watching for any knock-on effect at Ghana's pumps.

This is a fast-moving story. We'll update as it's confirmed.

(Source: CNN | Reuters | Bloomberg)
#GreaterNews #Iran #Oil #World

How worried are you about fuel prices? 👇

US forces struck Iran again Wednesday, hitting targets to stop attacks on ships in the Strait of Hormuz after the July 8 truce collapsed. Iran threatens to shut the strait; oil jumped then eased. Developing. (Source: CNN/Reuters) #GreaterNews #Iran
